text:
BOTTOM LINE — Risk-on, AI chips squeezed everything. SOXX ripped +6.62% and QQQ +2.51% as AI-semi momentum and headlines like Intel’s Apple chip partnership squeezed shorts and forced performance-chasing into high-beta tech.[6][MOVER INTC] Broad indices like SPY and DIA flat-lined despite 1.5x volume, signaling index supply against concentrated demand for semis and DRAM while factor data still show value regimes intact.[VOLUME BREADTH][FACTOR REGIME] Energy’s -1.65% giveback alongside gold/silver weakness and modest dollar strength framed this as a pure growth-chase day, not a macro or defensives-led move.[SECTORS][CROSS-ASSET]

INDICES — VIX slipped to 16.8, below its 20-day 17.4, confirming easing index-vol despite the tech ramp.[VOL SIGNALS] Return rank: SOXX +6.62% > QQQ +2.51% > IWM +1.97% > IJR +1.81% > IJH +1.07% > RSP +0.46% > DIA +0.00% > SPY +0.00% > GLD -0.38% > SLV -1.81%.[INDICES] STYLE: LARGE > SMALL by 0.54pp, with large +2.51% versus small +1.97%.[STYLE] Most notable cross-asset move was SLV -1.81% as metals bled further while UUP gained +0.43%.[CROSS-ASSET]

VOLUME — SPY traded at 1.4x average volume and QQQ’s explicit vol_ratio was not provided, but SOXX at 0.9x shows the biggest price winner rode normal-to-light liquidity, classic momentum chase rather than broad accumulation.[VOLUME BREADTH] DIA and IWM at 1.5x with modest price gains signal some catch-up interest in cyclicals and small caps, while low XLK 0.8x volume versus +3.04% is a low-conviction tech melt-up.[VOLUME BREADTH][SECTORS]

SECTORS & ROTATION — XLK Technology +3.04%, XLY Discretionary +1.45%, XLI Industrials +0.73%, XLU Utilities +0.67%, XLC Comms +0.23%, XLRE Real Estate -0.25%, XLB Materials -0.40%, XLP Staples -0.45%, XLV Healthcare -0.87%, XLF Financials -0.89%, XLE Energy -1.65%.[SECTORS] Top industries: DRAM +9.66%, SOXX +6.62%, SMH +5.76%, ARKG +5.19%; bottom: SLX -2.12%, GDX -2.19%, SILJ -2.34%, OIH -2.93%.[INDUSTRIES] Money in: semis and DRAM; money out: metals, energy services, and financials, consistent with AI enthusiasm and Iran-related oil softness.[TODAY'S MARKET CONTEXT][INDUSTRIES] Tape message is AI hardware leading.

POSITIONING — SPY PCR_vol 1.16 and QQQ 1.05 with positive skew show decent put demand and put premium, indicating protection is getting bought into the tech ramp.[OPTIONS INTEL] Spot above SPY and QQQ GEX_flip 750.0 suggests dealers are long gamma, dampening index-level swings even as single-name semis rip.[OPTIONS INTEL] VIX at 16.8 below its 20-day 17.4 and MOVE at 65.4 below its 20-day 72.5 show equity and bond vol are both in cooling territory.[VOL SIGNALS]

CROSS-ASSET — UUP +0.43% alongside GLD -0.38% and SLV -1.81% signals dollar strength pressuring precious metals while growth equities rally, a classic “real-yield/dollar up, metals down, tech up” chain.[CROSS-ASSET] USO only +0.56% with XLE -1.65% reflects equity investors fading Iran-risk-driven crude bounces and taking profits in an over-owned energy sector.[CROSS-ASSET][SECTORS]

SIGNALS & WATCH — Fear & Greed 58.9/100 sits neutral while KLAC +8.73% (The Fab 7), TER +7.19% (The Fab 7), and MRVL +7.27% (The Semi 7) led single-name action with AI-hardware leverage.[FEAR & GREED][VOL SPIKES] 20d momentum extremes were SOXX +22.90% on the upside and GLD -7.25% on the downside, with XLE -10.08% and DRAM +20d not provided but implied strong for the basket extremes.[INDICES][SECTORS][INDUSTRIES] KLAC 7.1x vol and price up is clean accumulation in inspection/CapEx, while GD -3.53% on 4.0x vol looks like defense-sector distribution.[VOL SPIKES] Intel’s +10.5% jump on Apple chip-partnership headlines anchored the AI supply-chain squeeze.[MOVER INTC] Watch MU +8.70% for follow-through in DRAM leadership, XLF -0.89% for potential crack in cyclical value, and ARKG +5.19% as high-beta biotech joins the AI-adjacent risk bucket.[MOVER MU][SECTORS][INDUSTRIES]
